headline: CFO Transition and Steady Dividend: What Is Progressive’s (PGR) Capital Return Playbook Signaling Now?
summary: Earlier this month, The Progressive Corporation’s board declared a US$0.10 per-share dividend payable on April 10, 2026, and confirmed that long-time Chief Financial Officer John Sauerland will retire on July 3, with Chief Strategy Officer Andrew Quigg set to assume the finance role. This combination of a maintained cash payout and a planned CFO handover highlights Progressive’s focus on continuity in capital returns and financial leadership after Sauerland’s 35-year tenure. With a planned...

headline: BMO Trims The Progressive Corporation (PGR) Price Target as Pricing Outlook Softens
summary: We recently compiled a list of the 10 Oversold Insurance Stocks to Buy According to Analysts. The Progressive Corporation (NYSE:PGR) is among the most oversold stocks. TheFly reported on March 11 that BMO Capital reduced its price target for PGR to $208 from $232 while keeping a Market Perform rating on the shares. The firm […]
